factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US Deal)
factors.summary: Major headline: Iran-US peace deal framework near completion with Hormuz set to reopen; multiple sources confirm signing in coming days. Risk-off de-escalation typically weighs on safe-haven demand for gold, though initial market reaction showed modest upside as inflation anxiety persists.

factors.name: Oil Price (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: US crude fell below $85 on Friday as Iran deal progress signaled Hormuz reopening; lower oil reduces near-term inflation input and removes geopolitical premium. This eases Fed pressure, which is initially gold-negative but longer-term bullish via lower real yields.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY lost ground during the week (down 0.27% to ~99.80) ahead of heavy central bank calendar; risk-on sentiment on Iran deal framework typically weakens the dollar. Weaker USD is a direct structural bullish factor for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in news window, but lower nominal yields expected if geopolitical premium unwinds and Fed holds or cuts. Without fresh CPI/inflation data, real yields remain the dominant unknown—critical to monitor for next 24-72h.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: No FOMC decision or Fed speak in latest news cycle. Upcoming central bank calendar (Fed, BoJ, BoE, RBA decisions) noted for next week; no hawkish surprise yet. This is a key upcoming catalyst that could shift bias sharply if Fed signals higher-for-longer rates.

factors.name: Inflation Data (CPI/PCE/PPI)
factors.summary: No recent inflation prints in news window. Social Security COLA for 2027 may jump to 4.7% per one estimate, signaling persistent inflation concerns, but this is secondary to actual CPI/PCE releases due this week or next.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Breakevens
factors.summary: Market participants remain optimistic about middle-east stabilization, but underlying inflation anxiety persists (noted in analyst commentary on gold clearing $4,200). 5y5y breakevens likely stable; no deflationary shock evident.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment (VIX, Equities)
factors.summary: Major indices closed higher on the day and week; SpaceX IPO success ($75B, +19% day-one) and Iran deal optimism fueled risk-on. However, gold also cleared $4,200 on Friday, suggesting dual safe-haven + real-yield support despite equity strength.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Bank Buying
factors.summary: No fresh data on GLD/IAU holdings or PBoC/RBI buying in this news cycle. Structural CB demand remains in place but not a near-term driver this week.

factors.name: Physical Demand Seasonality
factors.summary: No mention of Indian wedding season or Chinese holiday demand; out of primary seasonal bid windows for now.

screening.narrative: Gold has cleared and consolidated above $4,200 on Friday as market participants reconciled two opposing forces: (1) risk-off geopolitical de-escalation (Iran-US deal near signing, Hormuz reopening, oil down) which usually weighs on safe-haven demand, versus (2) persistent inflation anxiety and weakening USD as markets anticipate lower real yields if the Fed responds to falling oil/geopolitical premium with dovish bias. The 24h +0.66% gain and flatness in 4h (-0.04%) suggests the move is consolidating after a Phase 2 rally rather than extending. Upcoming central bank decisions (Fed, BoJ, BoE, RBA) and fresh inflation data (CPI/PCE likely this week) are critical wildcards that could flip the bias sharply.
catalysts_ahead: Fed FOMC decision & dot plot (likely mid-late June per calendar)
US CPI/Core CPI release (scheduled early-mid June)
PCE/Core PCE (may be released this week or next)
BoJ, BoE, RBA policy decisions (week of June 16-20)
Iran deal signing (next few days per Trump admin)
Oil market reaction to actual Hormuz supply flow changes
screening.risk_warning: If the Iran deal is formally signed AND holds, oil stabilizes below $85, and the Fed signals hawkish hold or rate hikes (not cuts) in response to sticky inflation data, gold could reverse sharply lower as real yields rise and USD strengthens. Conversely, if central banks pivot dovish or CPI surprises to the downside, gold could break higher past $4,250. The move is already +0.66% in 24h and consolidating, so late entries are higher-risk; wait for a pullback toward $4,215-$4,220 for better risk/reward into the central bank decision gauntlet.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 consolidation; if bullish bias holds, look for pullback retest of $4,215-$4,220 before considering fresh longs. Do not chase above $4,250 without confirmation of Fed dovishness or fresh CPI miss. Shorts risky until real yields data clarifies Fed stance post-Iran-deal.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh real yield data in news window. Prior read (BUY 6/10) suggests real yields were supportive, but no new print to confirm continuation or deterioration of that tailwind.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Guidance
factors.summary: No FOMC decision, dot plot, or fresh Powell/Williams/Waller commentary in past 24h. Upcoming week includes BoJ/BoE/RBA decisions (per FXStreet note), but Fed calendar not explicit in immediate window.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: FXStreet noted DXY lost 0.27% to ~99.80 level during prior week as investors prepared for central bank calendar. Weaker USD structurally bullish for gold, but move is modest and data is 1-2 days old.

factors.name: Nominal Treasury Yields
factors.summary: No fresh 10y nominal yield data or 2s10s curve commentary in current news batch. Critical missing data point for establishing yield headwind/tailwind.

factors.name: Inflation Data (CPI/PCE/PPI)
factors.summary: No new inflation prints in past 24h. Social Security COLA estimate suggests inflation climbing toward 4.7% for 2027, but this is forward-looking, not a surprise catalyst.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Middle East / Iran)
factors.summary: Iran-US ceasefire negotiations signal **significant de-escalation risk premium**: Trump administration indicates deal signing 'likely in coming days,' US struck Iranian drones while talks continue, and three parties (US/Iran/Pakistan) signal framework agreement close. Lower tail-risk of Strait of Hormuz closure is moderately bullish for gold as safe-haven demand recedes.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equity Action
factors.summary: US major indices closed higher on the day and week on Iran deal progress framework per investingLive. SPX strength typically correlates with risk-on, which can weigh on gold; however, if equities rally on **geopolitical de-escalation** rather than inflation surprise, gold can hold firm.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No new data on PBoC, RBI, Turkey, Poland gold purchases in news window. Structural bid remains in background but not a fresh catalyst.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows (GLD/IAU)
factors.summary: No holdings data for GLD or IAU in past 24h. Positioning unknown; cannot assess institutional accumulation/distribution.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: No fresh oil price data in news window. CFTC considering blocking CME's 24/7 oil contract is a regulatory note, not a market mover. Oil weakness would support disinflationary narrative, mildly bullish for gold longer-term.

factors.name: US Economic Data (NFP/ISM/Retail Sales)
factors.summary: No fresh NFP, ISM, retail sales, or GDP nowcasts in past 24h. FXStreet mentions 'retail sales, FOMC' as upcoming focus, implying data due in next 24-72h but not yet released.

factors.name: Competing Safe-Havens (BTC, CHF)
factors.summary: Bitcoin stalled below $64,375 resistance per note, and no CHF commentary. Risk flows remain mixed; Bitcoin weakness slightly reduces 'digital gold' competition pressure.

factors.name: Upcoming Central Bank Decisions
factors.summary: FXStreet headlines BoJ, BoE, RBA decisions for the coming week. BoJ especially material for USD/JPY and yen carry trade unwinding, which can affect gold; BoE/RBA less direct but could shift DXY. Data day(s) ahead, likely to create volatility.

screening.narrative: Gold is consolidating after a modest +0.66% 24h gain and +0.08% 4h move, with the prior BUY6/10 bias still in place but lacking fresh confirmation. The dominant macro driver today is **geopolitical de-escalation on Iran**: Trump administration signals a ceasefire deal is days away, which normally reduces safe-haven demand and can pressure gold. Offsetting this, the US Dollar remains slightly soft (~99.80 DXY), and real yields remain unknown in the current window—both potential supports. The week ahead is heavy on central bank decisions (BoJ, BoE, RBA) and economic data (retail sales, likely FOMC-adjacent volatility), creating chop risk and limiting conviction.
catalysts_ahead: BoJ, BoE, RBA policy decisions (this week, timing TBD)
FOMC meeting or Powell speech (if scheduled this week)
Iran-US ceasefire agreement signing (Trump said 'coming days,' risk of disappointment or surprise hawkishness)
CPI/PCE inflation print (if scheduled this week; currently unknown)
screening.risk_warning: This bias could invalidate if: (1) Iran deal collapses or is postponed → sharp safe-haven rally in gold; (2) inflation surprise (hot CPI/PCE) comes in higher than expected → real yields spike, gold sold hard; (3) Fed Chair or major central banker signals hawkishness → DXY rallies, gold pressured; (4) equity selloff on any negative catalyst → gold could rally as safe-haven but also get caught in risk-off deleveraging of long positions.
screening.entry_guidance: Gold is in PHASE_2 consolidation after modest overnight gains; move is not extended but lacks fresh confirmation. Recommend watching for a retest of $4,230 support with volume confirmation before adding long, or waiting for a break above $4,250 resistance on fresh DXY weakness or real yield data. Avoid chasing into central bank decision week without clear trigger.
